Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: 1. Understand the Normative Process Start Receive order Step 2 Check inventory must happen before payment fulfillment Branch 1 Sufficient Inventory Sequence Receive Check Authorize payment Package items Ship order Close order Branch 2 Insufficient Inventory Sequence Receive Check Cancel order Close order Constraints Rules Authorize payment must be after Check inventory and before Package items Package items must be after authorization and before shipping Ship order must happen after packaging Ship order should never happen after cancellation Close order must be final No fulfillment activities package ship after cancellation

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 2. Analyze the Event Log Case by Case Case 1 Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Package items Ship order Close order Analysis Matches the Sufficient Inventory flow perfectly Verdict Conformant Case 2 Receive order Check inventory Package items Authorize payment Ship order Close order Analysis Violation of the rule Package items should only occur after authorization and before shipping Packaging happened before authorization Verdict Non-conformant Violation Case 4 Receive order Check inventory Cancel order Close order Analysis Matches the Insufficient Inventory flow perfectly Verdict Conformant Case 5 Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Ship order Close order Analysis Skipped Package items Violates the sequence constraint that shipping requires prior packaging Verdict Non-conformant Violation

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Case 3 Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Cancel order Ship order Close order Analysis 1 Authorization followed by Cancellation The text doesnt explicitly forbid this e.g. maybe authorized but then customer cancelled though the standard flow is Check Cancel However the text explicitly says Once an order is canceled it would be illogical to continue with activities intended for fulfillment such as packaging or shipping 2 Shipping after cancellation is explicitly forbidden Verdict Non-conformant Violation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: 3. Synthesize the Output Need to list the violating cases and explain why based on the text provided Case 2 Violation of ordering between Authorization and Packaging Case 3 Violation of logical consistency regarding Cancellation vs Fulfillment Shipping after cancellation Case 5 Violation of missing mandatory step Packaging before Shipping Refining Explanations Case 2 The normative text states Package items should only occur after authorization In Case 2 Package items occurred at 10:00 while Authorize payment occurred at 10:30 Case 3 The normative text states Ship order never after a cancellation and it would be illogical to continue with shipping In Case 3 Cancel order happened at 10:00 yet Ship order happened later at 10:30 Case 5 The normative text outlines the expected flow as Receive Check Authorize Payment Package Ship Close Only after items have been properly packaged is it appropriate to proceed with Ship order Case 5 skips Package items entirely going straight from Authorize payment to Ship order

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Final check of the prompt requirements Identify which cases deviate Explain rules violated Resolve ambiguities though these cases seem fairly clear-cut violations of explicit statements in the text
